---
title: "Titanic: End-to-End Classification Case Study"
description: "End-to-end machine learning workflow for predicting passenger survival using structured tabular data."
---

## Overview

This project develops an end-to-end machine learning workflow for predicting passenger survival using the Kaggle Titanic dataset.

Rather than focusing solely on leaderboard performance, the project emphasizes the complete classification modeling process, including exploratory data analysis, feature engineering, feature transformations, model comparison, and rigorous evaluation through cross-validation.

The final objective is to build a predictive model capable of estimating the probability of survival for passengers in the test set and generating a valid Kaggle competition submission.

---

## Problem Statement

Given a set of passenger characteristics, the system must:

1. Explore and understand the dataset.
2. Prepare and engineer meaningful predictive features.
3. Train and compare multiple classification models.
4. Predict passenger survival for unseen observations.

---

## Methodology

The project follows a structured machine learning pipeline:

* Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)
* Data cleaning and missing value treatment
* Feature engineering
* Feature transformations
* Feature encoding
* Baseline models
* Logistic Regression
* Decision Tree
* Random Forest
* Gradient Boosting
* Cross-validation and model comparison
* Final model training and Kaggle submission

---

## Dataset

The project uses the **Titanic: Machine Learning from Disaster** dataset from Kaggle.

The dataset contains demographic, socioeconomic, and travel information for passengers aboard the RMS Titanic, including:

* Passenger demographics
* Ticket and fare information
* Passenger class
* Family relationships
* Port of embarkation
* Survival status (training set)

The objective is to predict the survival of passengers in the test set.

---

## Project Structure

The repository is organized as follows:

```text
kaggle-titanic/
│
├── README.md                                   # Project overview and usage instructions.
├── index.qmd                                   # Landing page for the Quarto website.
├── _quarto.yml                                 # Quarto website configuration.
├── environment.yml                             # Conda environment specification.
├── LICENSE                                     # Project license.
│
├── notebooks/
│   ├── 01_exploratory_data_analysis.ipynb      # Exploratory analysis of the Titanic dataset.
│   ├── 02_feature_engineering.ipynb            # Feature engineering, transformations, and encoding.
│   └── 03_modeling_and_evaluation.ipynb        # Model training, evaluation, and Kaggle submission.
│
├── data/
│   ├── raw/
│   │   ├── train.csv                           # Kaggle training dataset.
│   │   ├── test.csv                            # Kaggle test dataset.
│   │   └── gender_submission.csv               # Example Kaggle submission file.
│   │
│   └── processed/
│       ├── 01_data.parquet                     # Dataset produced after exploratory data analysis.
│       ├── 01_features.parquet                 # Feature registry after exploratory data analysis.
│       ├── 02_data.parquet                     # Dataset after feature engineering.
│       ├── 02_features.parquet                 # Updated feature registry after feature engineering.
│       └── submission.csv                      # Final predictions submitted to Kaggle.
│
└── _site/
    └── ...                                     # Website generated by Quarto.
```

---

## Status

This project documents a complete supervised classification workflow, from raw data exploration to model evaluation and Kaggle submission generation.
